summary: Great soundboard recording with crisp audio, highlighted by energetic China Rider, Crazy Fingers and a standout Minglewood jam.
review: The show is remembered as one of the best from the Europe‑90 tour – high energy, tight playing and memorable moments like China Rider opening the set, a soaring Crazy Fingers and an ecstatic Minglewood. The soundboard source captures the venue’s ambience well; reviewers call it “great” and “crisp,” with only minor imperfections noted.

ai_rating.rationale: Most of the 17 substantive reviews rate the performance 4‑5 stars, praising both musicianship and sound quality; only a few note minor flaws, giving an overall strong positive consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, great‑sounding and well‑balanced. A couple of reviewers mention the sound isn’t perfect (volume turned down for venue size), but no major dropouts or distortion are reported.

poor_songs: Crazy Fingers (inconsistent performance noted by one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is praised for its upbeat vibe and tight transitions, especially the opening China Rider and the high‑energy second half. A few reviewers felt the energy dipped later in the second set, describing some jams as lazy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead guitar shines on Sugaree, Crazy Fingers and Minglewood; several reviewers call his playing “textbook Dead” and “especially on.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work is highlighted as amazing by one reviewer; otherwise noted as solid throughout the night.
band_member_comments.Keys: The new keyboardists (Vince & Bruce) are mentioned filling in after Brent’s death, adding honky‑tonk piano flavor and keeping the sound full.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drum segment and “NFA” (no‑fun?) praised; overall drum work considered tight.
